title: A Village Visit: Eklutna
summary: Throughout 2015, this newsletter will showcase a series of profiles on the seven ANCSA-certified villages in the Cook Inlet region. We’ll take a look at what life is like in each village: the attractions, the opportunities, the challenges. Our second village is Eklutna. Read More The post A Village...

title: Spotlight: Debbie Fullenwider
summary: Local perspective on Eklutna Photo by Andre Horton. “I haven’t retired. I keep hearing that rumor, but I never said I was retiring,” insists Debbie Fullenwider. Since 1973, she’s served in a variety of leadership positions on the Eklutna Inc. board of directors, including president and vice president. “I was the first president of the Eklutna...

title: Dead elk don’t wander
summary: North Wind elk study keeps client in compliance Aerial crews prepare to conduct a wildlife capture as part of a North Wind Resource Consulting project. Photos courtesy of Tim Lingle. Tim Lingle has a few undead elk on his hands. “We get a lot of false notifications that elk have died,” Lingle explains over the phone from his office in Pinedale, Wyo. As the Wyoming operations manager for CIRI subsidiary North Wind Resource Consulting, it’s his job to keep tabs on the 46 GPS-collared elk that roam t...

title: Federal loan program seeks Alaska Native homebuyers
summary: Thinking of buying a home this year? Then think about looking into the HUD 184 Loan Program. This federal program offers housing opportunities for Alaska Native and American Indian people and eligible tribal entities. title: U.S. Farmers Expect to Plant Record-High Soybean Acreage Again
summary: For a second year in a row, U.S. growers are raising the bar on soybean acreage. Growers across the United States intend to plant an estimated record-high 84.6 million acres of soybeans in 2015, up 1 percent from the previous record set in 2014, according to the Prospective Plantings report released...
